records.title: 47 CFR Part 18 -- Industrial, Scientific, and Medical Equipment
records.authors_or_org: U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC)
records.claim_supported: FCC Part 18 Section 18.301 defines the designated ISM (industrial, scientific, medical) frequency bands: 6.78, 13.56, 27.12, 40.68, 915, 2450, 5800 MHz and 24.125-245.00 GHz, each with a stated tolerance (e.g., 915 MHz +/-13.0 MHz, 2450 MHz +/-50.0 MHz) -- the bands used by essentially all industrial magnetron heating/curing/drying equipment in this lane. ISM equipment may also operate on any frequency above 9 kHz except as restricted elsewhere in the part.
records.notes: Official current regulation text (eCFR, government-hosted); page found via search, not independently WebFetched in full this session. | Canonical citation URL (ecfr.gov/current/.../part-18) retained for the record identity even though the API-endpoint variant was the one actually fetched successfully this session; both resolve to the same official regulation text.

records.title: ISO 11137-1:2025 -- Sterilization of health care products -- Radiation -- Part 1: Requirements for the development, validation and routine control of a sterilization process for medical devices
records.authors_or_org: International Organization for Standardization (ISO)
records.claim_supported: Second edition (2025) of ISO 11137-1 cancels and replaces the first edition (ISO 11137-1:2006), covering radiation sterilization (Co-60/Cs-137 gamma, electron-beam, X-ray) validation and routine control for medical devices. Key 2025 changes: allowable electron-beam energy raised from 10 MeV to 11 MeV and X-ray energy from 5 MeV to 7.5 MeV; ISO 13004 and ASTM 52628 added as normative references; dose-audit frequency shifted from quarterly to every four months (4/year); VDmax dose options expanded in 2.5 kGy increments; dosimetry-testing language clarified to allow parametric/machine-based batch release in well-controlled facilities.
records.notes: Standard itself is paywalled; bibliographic/scope detail taken from ISO catalogue page and secondary summaries (International Irradiation Association news item) found via WebSearch. Not independently WebFetched in full this session. | ISO's own catalogue/purchase page is bot-blocked (403, confirmed on two attempts); scope and edition content instead independently confirmed via IIA (a recognized industry trade association), which is an acceptable venue-content confirmation source. The standard's identity (ISO 11137-1:2025) itself is not in doubt.

records.title: IEC 60060-1:2025 -- High-voltage test techniques -- Part 1: General terminology and test requirements
records.authors_or_org: International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
records.claim_supported: Fourth edition (published 17 April 2025, 164 pages, maintained by IEC Technical Committee 42) of IEC 60060-1 cancels and replaces the third edition (2010), defining terminology and test requirements for dielectric tests with direct, alternating, impulse, and combined/composite high voltages for equipment with Um above 1.0 kV AC / 1.5 kV DC. Key updates: extended positive tolerance of lightning-impulse front time for Um > 800 kV; new Annex C (manual calculation from graphical waveforms) and informative Annex F (new front-time definition for switching-impulse voltage).
records.notes: Standard itself is paywalled; bibliographic/scope detail from ANSI/GlobalSpec catalogue listings found via WebSearch. Not independently WebFetched in full this session. | URL swapped from the scout's ANSI-reseller page (webstore.ansi.org, 403 on two attempts) to the official IEC publisher page, which fetched successfully -- same canonical standard identity (standard:iec-60060-1-2025), preferable since it is the primary publisher rather than a reseller.
